beats.narration_text: Merhaba — this is Liam, in for Bear. You are interacting with Google Drive through the Drive REST API v3. The skill gives you two base hosts, four things to know before the first request, and two bundled scripts that handle the hardest parts. The model: everything in Drive is a file — including folders. The trap: Workspace files have no bytes. Let me walk you through the architecture and the gaps.

props.command: Search Drive for last quarter's reports and read the summary doc.
output: Two base hosts: metadata + search vs upload host
Everything is a file — folders via parents[], no path API
Workspace files: no bytes — must export, not alt=media

beats.narration_text: The skill opens with four things to know before the first request. One: everything is a file, including folders. A folder is a file with a specific mime type. Hierarchy is the parents array. There is no path API. You navigate by ID. Two: Google Workspace files — Docs, Sheets, Slides — have no downloadable bytes. An alt=media request returns four-oh-three fileNotDownloadable. You must export them to a concrete format. Binary files download directly. Three: responses only include the fields you ask for. The default subset is minimal and omits nextPageToken. Always pass fields= explicitly. Four: files on a shared drive are invisible unless you pass supportsAllDrives equals true on every request — per-file calls and list calls have different parameter sets. The most common surprise four-oh-four is a missing supportsAllDrives. The skill also provides two bundled scripts: drive_search.sh handles the q expression, pagination, and all-drives access. drive_read.sh branches on mime type — exporting Workspace files, downloading everything else — and guards against large binaries.

props.sparkLine: Everything is a file. Workspace files have no bytes. fields= or nextPageToken silently vanishes.

beats.act: design — pagination trap + Workspace export + shared drive 404
beats.narration_text: Three design points. The fields= parameter is the trap that compounds everything. The default response omits nextPageToken. A listing that returns exactly one hundred files is not a complete result — it is a silent truncation. You only discover this if you explicitly request nextPageToken in fields= and get it back. The drive_search.sh script handles this correctly; raw curl calls are where you forget. Workspace file export is the second design point. The mime type determines whether you use alt=media or the export endpoint. Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Drawings go through export; every binary format goes through alt=media with a four-oh-three if you get them mixed up. drive_read.sh branches on this for you. The shared drive design point is the third: files on a shared drive return four-oh-four as if they don't exist. The fix is supportsAllDrives=true. For list and search requests it's a three-parameter set: corpora=allDrives, includeItemsFromAllDrives=true, supportsAllDrives=true. The skill documents this. The gap is that the error message says not found — not wrong parameters.

props.sparkLine: fields= silently drops nextPageToken. Workspace files: export path, not alt=media. Shared drive 404 = missing param.

beats.act: teardown — what it gets right / where it bites
beats.narration_text: Here is the teardown. The skill gets five things right. The two-host architecture — metadata host and upload host — is stated before any operations. You know before the first curl which base URL to use. The Workspace files no-bytes trap is explained before the operation list, not buried in error codes — it prevents the most common four-oh-three before you can make it. The two bundled scripts have exhaustive flag documentation with real example invocations — drive_search.sh builds q expressions, follows pagination, includes all drives; drive_read.sh branches on mime type and guards large binaries. The shared drive four-oh-four is named the most common surprise four-oh-four and given the full three-parameter fix — right diagnosis, right location. Rate limits are documented in quota units, not request counts, with per-operation costs — calibrates how aggressively to iterate. Where it bites. The nextPageToken omission from the default fields= subset is documented in the Pagination section, separated from the operation recipes. The drive_search.sh note is buried after the q-expression documentation. It is easy to write a raw files.list call and miss it entirely. The gdrive helper function is session-only — defined once in a code block, not a persisted script. The skill references it across all operations but it won't survive a new terminal session. Export size limit: exportSizeLimitExceeded is named as an error but the SKILL.md has no fallback — deferred to references/api.md. The multipart upload content-type discrepancy — multipart/related versus multipart/form-data — is documented with a caveat but no resolution recipe, only a sentence saying use explicit boundary with data-binary. And pageSize cap of one hundred silently clamps larger values with no warning at the point of use.

props.sparkLine: Two-host model and Workspace no-bytes trap nailed. fields= nextPageToken gap: easy to miss.

beats.narration_text: Verdict. google-drive-api: two base hosts, metadata and upload. Everything is a file including folders — hierarchy via parents[], no path API. Workspace files have no bytes — export to text, csv, or pdf. fields= must include nextPageToken or pagination silently stops at one page. supportsAllDrives=true required for shared drive files — missing it returns four-oh-four. Bundled scripts: drive_search.sh and drive_read.sh. Rate limits: quota units per operation, not request counts. Gaps: nextPageToken default omission — documented but separated from recipes. gdrive helper is session-only. Export size fallback deferred. Multipart content-type caveat without resolution. pageSize cap without warning.

artifactLines: Two base hosts: metadata/search vs upload host (googleapis.com/upload/...)
Everything is a file — folders are mimeType=folder, hierarchy via parents[]
Workspace files (Docs/Sheets/Slides): no bytes — must export, not alt=media
fields= must include nextPageToken or pagination silently truncates at 100
Shared drive 404 = missing supportsAllDrives=true (+ 2 more params for list)
Gaps: nextPageToken split from recipes · gdrive helper session-only · export fallback deferred

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Ask Claude to find all spreadsheets modified in the last 30 days on a shared drive and export the first one as CSV. Watch five things. First: does it use drive_search.sh with the modifiedTime q clause, or does it write a raw curl that forgets to include nextPageToken in fields=? Second: does it pass all three shared drive parameters — corpora, includeItemsFromAllDrives, supportsAllDrives — on the search? Third: does it branch on mimeType before deciding whether to use alt=media or export? Fourth: does it use the gdrive() helper for subsequent calls, or does it write new bearer headers from scratch? Fifth: if the export returns exportSizeLimitExceeded, does it stop and report, or try to recover without guidance from the skill?

props.command: Find all spreadsheets modified in the last 30 days on our shared drive and export the first one as CSV.
props.runningText: Watch: fields= nextPageToken? supportsAllDrives triple-param? mimeType branch? export fallback?
output: Expected: drive_search.sh with modifiedTime + all 3 shared-drive params
Expected: export branch for Sheets → text/csv via drive_read.sh
Red flag: raw curl missing nextPageToken · alt=media on a Sheet · missing supportsAllDrives
